task_description: Resolve new merge conflicts in the local git branch for warpdotdev/warp PR #11448. Fetch updated base branch origin/main, merge or rebase the feature branch, identify conflicted files (Rust .rs source plus possibly Cargo.lock / config), resolve each conflict by reconciling both sides intent, then verify with cargo build/clippy/tests before pushing.

Implement issue #908: modify src/claude_configs/deploy.py build_plan Phase 2 to add an empty-directory-pruning pass that emits removal plan items for directories left empty after orphan-file deletion, scoped to tracked_top_levels. Add dry-run preview visibility and write two negative-case unit tests. Verify with pytest and ruff.

Rename the Claude Code skill session-variance to session-analysis in the claude-prospector repo. Harness edit: rename skills/session-variance/ directory to skills/session-analysis/, update the name: field in SKILL.md frontmatter, update references in README.md.

Implement issue #890: add a deploy-after-tag guard in src/claude_configs/deploy.py that refuses to deploy when HEAD is not a released tagged commit and there are un-released commits touching deployable paths since the last tag. Print a clear error naming the last tag and commit count, and provide an --allow-unreleased escape hatch. Add unit tests. Verify with pytest and ruff.

Implement issue #901 option 1: add a deterministic CHANGELOG link-ref verification step to the release runbook docs/release-process.md. After editing CHANGELOG during a release, a grep-based check asserts the [Unreleased] compare-base and the new [X.Y.Z] link-ref both resolve, aborting the commit step if not. Documentation/prose edit to a runbook markdown file.

Plan the drift-aggregation feature for claude-prospector (issue #63 remaining ACs): a new deterministic CLI command that reads accumulated variance/<id>.json records and reports drift frequency, severity distribution, and trend over a configurable time window, plus extending the session-analysis skill to surface the trend view. Produce an implementation spec/plan. New multi-part feature that needs scoping and phasing into a plan.

Investigate why a repo/plugin is not appearing in the GitHub marketplace. First step: check the status of the last GitHub Actions workflow runs (CI/release runs) to determine when the last update/publish occurred. Read-only GitHub query — list recent workflow runs and their timestamps/conclusions.

Investigate why a specific GitHub repo is not being discovered by the buildwithclaude Discover Plugins scraper. Root cause unknown. Read the scraper source code and GitHub Actions workflow in the local checkout to understand its discovery criteria (GitHub search query, topics, file markers, manifest) then determine why the user repo fails to match. Read-heavy investigation spanning scraper logic and its data/config.

Read-only GitHub query: gather facts about repo glitchwerks/claude-plugins to check against scraper discovery criteria. Need repo metadata (fork? private? archived? stars? topics?) and presence/validity of manifest files on default branch at .claude-plugin/plugin.json, root marketplace.json, root plugin.json. Also whether it surfaces via GitHub code search and topic search.

Write a durable investigation findings document (Markdown root-cause report / design doc) capturing a completed evidence-backed investigation. Content is already known and provided; this is faithful transcription and formatting into a clean documentation file, not new research.

Prepare release v1.2.0: bump version string 1.1.1 to 1.2.0 in pyproject.toml and .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and add a new CHANGELOG.md section documenting the Snowball/Porter2 stemming feature, trigger-algorithm research doc, and dispatch-authoring skill update. Edit files pyproject.toml, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, CHANGELOG.md. Keep a Changelog format.

Remove dead context-switch: true frontmatter line from skills/gh-pr-review-address/SKILL.md in the claude-github-tools plugin. One-line deletion in a skill SKILL.md frontmatter. This is a harness edit to a skill SKILL.md file.
implementing a new feature

Create a GitHub issue documenting two plugin bugs: run_gh_api() in scripts/_gh_common.py unconditionally json.loads gh stdout and fails on raw scalar --jq output; and gh-release-status.py missing from working tree. GitHub write operation, create issue.

Read and review the change-request feedback on GitHub PR #11723 in warpdotdev/warp. Fetch the PR details, all review comments (inline plus conversation), requested-changes reviews, and CI status so I can understand what actionable feedback needs addressing. Read-only GitHub query.

Verify a code-review claim by tracing Rust input-handling behavior on PR branch cbeaulieu-gt/feat-rich-input-ctrl-enter. Reviewer claims that when the submit_on_ctrl_enter toggle is OFF, Ctrl+Enter in the Rich Input editor becomes a no-op, whereas the existing default behavior is that both Shift+Enter and Ctrl+Enter insert a newline. Determine whether this regression claim is true by reading the EnterSettings and EnterAction logic and tracing how Ctrl+Enter is handled when the toggle is false. Read-only investigation and verification of a behavioral claim, no fix yet, behavior is uncertain.

Read-only inspection and review of the Rust test files added in a PR. Read every test in input_tests.rs and rich_input_ctrl_enter.rs, group them by purpose, build a coverage matrix mapping each test to its behavior cell and layer (unit/integration), flag regression tests and cross-layer/permutation duplicate tests, and produce a keep/cut recommendation per test valuing common-path cases over extreme corner cases. No code edits, this is a classification and analysis report to inform which tests to trim.

Implement a known-cause bug fix and apply an agreed test refactor on a PR branch in a Rust repo. Fix: make the ctrl_enter EnterAction conditional on the submit_on_ctrl_enter toggle in update_cli_agent_enter_settings, and re-invoke it on the settings-change handler. Add a regression test for the toggle-OFF Ctrl+Enter inserts-newline behavior. Then apply a pre-approved keep/cut list to the Rust test files, cutting corner-case and cross-layer-duplicate tests, merging two integration tests into one, keeping regression guards, and trim verbose comments. Edit Rust code and tests. Do not push.

Check pre-merge state of three open GitHub PRs #760 #761 #762 in glitchwerks/job-matcher: CI check rollup status, CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews, unresolved inline review comments, general conversation comments, review-bot output. Read-only GitHub queries.

GitHub read-only query for /whats-next recap: for repo warpdotdev/warp fetch PR #11723 status (state, isDraft, mergedAt, title, body, CI check rollup, review states especially advait-m CHANGES_REQUESTED) and issue #11588 title/body/state. Read-only fire-and-forget.
Read-only pre-push safety check: confirm PR #11723 on warpdotdev/warp is still OPEN and not merged before I push to the fork branch. Quick GitHub read fire-and-forget.

Trim verbose comments in app/src/terminal/input_tests.rs (Rust unit test file), behavior-preserving cleanup: drop the divider banner blocks above each test in the rich-input region, collapse or remove multi-line doc comments on tests whose names already describe them, remove inline comments that merely restate assertion messages. Keep the one regression why-it-fails-pre-fix note. Then verify by mirroring CI: cargo fmt check, cargo clippy all-targets tests, build.

Extract three situational sections out of the global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md harness file into standards/*.md files with @import lines, and move Python invocation/uv detail into the python skill, to reduce always-loaded context. Edit CLAUDE.md, create standards files, edit skills/python/SKILL.md. Harness/agent-authoring frontmatter doc edit.

Fix documentation file docs/maintenance/release-process.md: replace all occurrences of the repository name glitchwerks/plugins with the canonical renamed name glitchwerks/claude-plugins. Prose edit, markdown file, no code. Resolves issue #292.

Behavior-preserving comment trim across PR source files: aggressively trim verbose test-helper docstrings in step.rs, condense public API docs in editor/view/mod.rs, de-duplicate but keep the why in production logic comments in input.rs. Comment/docstring only no logic change. Verify mirroring CI cargo fmt check, clippy all-targets tests, build.

Check the user open pull request in the current GitHub repo, identify which PR is theirs, and determine its merge-conflict status. The user reports a merge conflict again and wants it resolved. Read-only GitHub query first to locate the PR and confirm the conflict, then likely a git rebase or merge to resolve conflicts.

Read-only GitHub state for repo glitchwerks/claude-wayfinder on branch main: fetch open issues #293 #305 #292, merge state of PR #313 and PR #312, status of Renovate PR #301, and any other currently-open issues and PRs. Format and return results.

Resolve a git merge conflict in a single Rust file (app/src/integration_testing/input/step.rs). The conflict is in the use-import block: both branches rewrote imports. Resolve by taking the union of imports the merged file body actually references, dedupe, complete the merge commit, and verify the crate compiles with no unused-import warnings (cargo check / clippy, repo uses -D warnings).

Resolve git merge conflicts across three Rust source files in crates/repo_metadata (entry.rs, local_model.rs with 7 hunks, local_model_tests.rs). These are logic conflicts where the feature branch changes collide with upstream changes to the same watcher model code. Must integrate both sides intent per hunk, complete the merge commit, and verify the crate compiles and its tests pass with clippy -D warnings.

Update the glitchwerks/job-matcher.wiki page Plugin-Development.md (a GitHub wiki backed by a separate git repo) to match the required/optional source-listing schema split documented in docs/PLUGIN_DEVELOPMENT.md merged in #515. Prose documentation sync: rewrite the wiki page schema table to split required vs optional canonical listing keys.

Create a GitHub milestone 0.1.0 Pilot harness and decompose the pilot harness scope from design docs into well-scoped GitHub issues assigned to that milestone, including the H1 label-reconciliation bug fix. GitHub-write operational work: create issues, create milestone, write issue bodies.

Implement issue #1: scaffold the pilot harness repo directory structure (bin/, scripts/, config/, templates/) and write the bin/run.sh launcher shell script in bash. The launcher resolves the harness dir, exports it, cd into the project repo, and runs baton start -w <config>. Write README.md and .gitignore. Verify all shell scripts with shellcheck. New code authoring.

List the current users open pull requests in the warpdotdev/warp GitHub repository, including PR numbers, titles, branch names, and changed files. Read-only GitHub query to identify which PRs need comment trimming.

Trim verbose multi-paragraph code comments in Rust source files to match a concise one-line comment style. Behavior-preserving cleanup: remove redundant rationale, issue-archaeology, and reviewer-attribution from // and /// comments while keeping code, cfg attributes, and test names unchanged.

Implement issue #2: write two bash lifecycle hook scripts for the Baton harness — after-create.sh (per-worktree dependency install: npm/pip) and before-run.sh (rebase worktree branch onto origin/main). Standalone independently-testable shell scripts that derive issue number from basename PWD. Verify with shellcheck and bash -n. New code authoring.

Implement issue #3: write the after-run.sh bash outcome router for the Baton harness. Classify the run terminal state (uncommitted-changes, no-commits, committed-no-pr, pr-opened) by inspecting git and GitHub, and reconcile GitHub issue labels to a single human-facing state. Standalone independently-testable shell script with comprehensive logging. Verify with shellcheck and bash -n. New code authoring.

Edit an existing implementation plan markdown file to add a new backend-less Slice P (Static Shell-B UI prototype) parallel to and independent of Slice 0/Cycle 0/Cycle 1. Update slice descriptions, the dependency graph, the sequencing statement, and the proposed-sub-issues table to integrate it coherently. Prose/planning doc edit.

Update design documentation markdown files to record the harness implementation-language decision (shell to Python) and reflect it in the repo structure and component descriptions. Edit docs/harness-design.md, add a brief consistency note to docs/architecture-spec.md, and a forward note to docs/spike-findings.md. Prose/documentation editing, not code.

Implement issue #10: scaffold the Python project foundation for the harness. Write pyproject.toml with uv-managed venv, ruff (lint+format), pytest, and optional mypy config; create the src/baton_harness package layout with hook entry points; add a GitHub Actions CI workflow running ruff and pytest; add Python .gitignore entries. New Python code authoring following PEP8, type hints, Google docstrings.

Implement issue #2 in Python: fill in the after_create.py and before_run.py hook entry-point stubs in src/baton_harness. after_create installs per-worktree deps (npm/pip via subprocess); before_run rebases the worktree branch onto origin/main and aborts on conflict. Use the existing _cli helpers, add pytest tests, pass ruff/mypy/pytest gate. Python code authoring.

Implement issue #3 in Python: fill in the after_run.py outcome-router stub in src/baton_harness. Classify the run terminal state (F5: uncommitted-changes, no-commits, committed-no-pr, pr-opened) via git and gh, parsing gh --json with json.loads, and reconcile GitHub issue labels to a single state. Use _cli helpers, add pytest tests, pass ruff/mypy/pytest gate. Python code authoring.

Read-only GitHub query: check open state and acceptance-criteria checkboxes of issue #63, list any open PR whose head branch is 63-drift-aggregation, check whether a Phase 0 timestamp producer PR exists open or merged against main in glitchwerks/claude-prospector. Return raw results.

Implement baton-harness issue #5 with a YAGNI scope change: remove per-project config scaffolding and flatten to a single generic config. Refactor the merged shell launcher bin/run.sh to drop its project-name argument and point at a fixed config/WORKFLOW.md; delete the config/example-project/ directory; author a new generic config/WORKFLOW.md (Baton yaml-frontmatter config plus agent-prompt body) and templates/CLAUDE.md.template skeleton with fill-in slots; reconcile docs harness-design.md, README.md, architecture-spec.md. Mostly markdown and config authoring plus one small shell edit to run.sh. Run full CI gate ruff mypy pytest.

Implement issue #214: automate GitHub Release creation in the GitHub Actions release workflow. Add a job to .github/workflows/release.yml that runs after publish-pypi succeeds and creates a GitHub Release for the pushed tag, extracting release notes from the matching CHANGELOG.md section and marking the newest as latest. Also update docs/release-process.md runbook to document the step and add a footguns entry, and add a CHANGELOG Unreleased note.

Find which of the current users open pull requests has a merge conflict (mergeable CONFLICTING / mergeStateStatus DIRTY). Read-only GitHub query: list open PRs authored by the user, check each PRs mergeable status. Return the PR number, title, branch, and base branch of the conflicting PR.

Resolve git merge conflicts in three Rust source files in crates/repo_metadata after merging upstream/master into a feature branch. Both sides changed the signature of compute_file_tree_mutations and the watcher registration code path; integrate both intents so the code compiles and preserves the PR feature. Verify by running the presubmit script (cargo clippy workspace all-targets, cargo test). Edit the .rs files to resolve conflict markers.

Verify feasibility of injecting autonomous-mode policy as a system-level prompt when the Baton orchestrator invokes the Claude Code claude binary. Confirm against official Anthropic Claude Code documentation whether the claude CLI supports an --append-system-prompt flag and whether it injects at system-prompt authority outranking project CLAUDE.md, including headless -p interaction; do not answer from memory, cite docs. Also read the upstream mraza007/baton GitHub repository source to determine how Baton constructs the claude subprocess invocation, how the prompt is passed, how command and permission_mode from WORKFLOW.md map onto it, and whether arbitrary extra CLI flags can be passed through via WORKFLOW.md config or require an upstream code change. Read-only investigation of platform docs and an external GitHub repo source.

Check CI/checks status of PR #17 on glitchwerks/baton-harness (gh pr checks rollup, review comments, CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews, review-bot output) and if all green and no blocking feedback merge the PR via squash. GitHub read of PR state plus GitHub write merge.
Read GitHub issue #6 on glitchwerks/baton-harness in full (title, body, acceptance criteria checkboxes, labels) so the user can review the next work item before starting. Read-only GitHub query.

Edit skills/python/SKILL.md to add a warning that ruff format --check can mask content issues behind CRLF line endings on Windows. Harness skill SKILL.md edit, documentation enhancement for issue #924.

Edit agents/debugger.md to add a Software Standards section ending in @import of standards/software-standards.md, mirroring the #918 change to code-writer and code-reviewer agent definitions. Harness agent-file edit.

Implement GitHub issue #776: build a static, backend-less Shell-B UI prototype for the job-matcher Flask app. Create real Jinja2 HTML templates (templates/*.html) fed hardcoded Python fixture context (no DB, no service layer). Deliver a three-region CSS grid layout, left rail role quick-switcher with localStorage, view-aware context bar, static screen renders for feed cards/candidate profile/roles editor/job preferences/inspector/admin against fixtures following docs/design/data-model.md, and a /prototype route tree in the existing Flask app. Follow docs/design/ui-layout.md, design-principles.md, docs/STYLE_GUIDE.md (no hardcoded hex, semantic tier colors). Verify CI green: ruff check ., djlint templates/ --lint, pytest. Net-new frontend code: HTML, CSS, Jinja2 templates, Python Flask routing.

Edit standards/software-standards.md to delete the Agent Instructions routing block of 3 bullets referencing nonexistent agents at the top of the file. Behavior-shaping standards content imported into agent bodies and CLAUDE.md.

Promote an already-authored, syntax-checked bash handoff script from .tmp/ to scripts/pilot-dry-run.sh in the baton-harness repo: create a feature branch + worktree, git-add the file with exec bit, commit referencing issue #6, push, open a PR (no Closes keyword). Git plumbing for an existing artifact.

Fix 4 identified PR review findings in the job-matcher Shell-B prototype (known root causes): (1) combined feed view renders cards unscored because annotation loop keys on a literal combined role_id in web/prototype.py; (2) resume fixtures omit the required content_ref field per data-model.md in web/prototype_fixtures.py; (3) verdict string concatenated raw into innerHTML in templates/prototype/_shell.html should use textContent; (4) add a combined-view regression test in tests/test_prototype.py. Then re-run ruff, djlint, pytest.

Read source of mraza007/baton (the symphony package) to find how it executes WORKFLOW.md lifecycle hooks (after_create/before_run/after_run): the exact command/shell it spawns, whether it uses a login shell (bash -lc), and whether it passes/sanitizes the environment (PATH) to the hook subprocess. GitHub read of external repo source.

Fix two Baton hook-integration bugs in the baton-harness Python package found during pilot validation: (1) resolve_issue_number in src/baton_harness/_cli.py must accept Baton bare-integer worktree names; (2) committed config/WORKFLOW.md hooks + bin/run.sh must not depend on PATH because Baton runs hooks via bash -lc login shell. Update docstrings, error messages, and add regression tests. Mirror CI: ruff, mypy, pytest.
Read-only GitHub query: list open issues in glitchwerks/claude-configs and surface the next release-worthy backlog item to work on next

Read authoritative GitHub state of issue #2 on cbeaulieu-gt/promptsmith — current labels, recent comments — and list open PRs to confirm a draft PR exists for the worktree branch. Diagnosing why after_run reported adding agent-done but the label is not visible. GitHub read.
Read-only GitHub query: fetch pre-merge review state for a pull request including formal reviews, inline comments, conversation comments, and mergeable status

Add target-repo label preflight to bin/run.sh and harden after_run label reconciliation against infinite re-dispatch loops in the baton-harness Python package, on existing branch fix/6-baton-hook-integration. Edit bin/run.sh (shell) and src/baton_harness/after_run.py (python) plus tests. Mirror CI: ruff, mypy, pytest.

Write a pilot validation findings note as a Markdown document in docs/ for baton-harness issue #6, documenting the T1/Scenario A/T2 dry-run results and five findings from running the harness live against cbeaulieu-gt/promptsmith. Prose documentation deliverable.

Fix a bug in bin/run.sh label preflight on branch fix/6-baton-hook-integration: it uses gh -C <dir> which is invalid (gh has no -C flag), causing the launch to crash. Replace with gh label list -R <owner/repo> --json name --jq, deriving the repo slug from the project repo git remote. Shell script fix.

Check CI/checks status of PR #20 on glitchwerks/baton-harness — gh pr checks rollup on the actual head commit, unresolved review comments, CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews, review-bot output. Read-only GitHub query to gate a merge.
List open issues on glitchwerks/baton-harness to determine the next issue to work on — read-only GitHub query, ordered by issue number, with labels and milestone.

Edit skills/bicep/SKILL.md and skills/azure/SKILL.md to add Azure/bicep footgun sections (bicepparam BCP258 strict assignment + az --tenant command discipline) per issue #494. Harness skill-body edits.

Split .github/workflows/ci.yml single sequential gate job into three parallel independent jobs: lint (ruff check + ruff format --check), type-check (mypy src), test (pytest). Use a composite action or reusable setup to avoid duplicating the uv install block. Edit GitHub Actions YAML workflow file.

Search existing GitHub issues on the upstream repo mraza007/baton for any report of blocked issues being re-dispatched across continuation turns, exclude_labels not being re-checked between turns, before_run only running once, or block-cost equals max_turns. Include open AND closed issues, especially any closed as rejected/wontfix. Read-only GitHub query.

Read-only GitHub state query for /whats-next recap in repo glitchwerks/claude-prospector: list open pull requests especially headRefName 63-drift-aggregation, and read issue #63 drift-aggregation umbrella including open acceptance-criteria checkboxes and state. Fire-and-forget GitHub read.

Remove the resolved TODO(#4) markers in src/baton_harness/after_run.py block path and add a regression test in tests/test_after_run.py asserting the block-path single-state invariant (blocked present -> agent-ready removed, agent-done NOT added, exit 0; plus partial-failure case). Python code edit with pytest verification.

Document the terminal-block design decision in docs/harness-design.md section 8 (and the H-note in docs/spike-findings.md): block is not terminal at the Baton level, the harness enforces the single-state label invariant but cannot stop continuation re-dispatch, block cost is approximately max_turns and is accepted for the pilot, upstream-dependent fix tracked separately. Prose documentation edit to design docs.

Fix GitHub Actions CI failure: local composite action uses ./.github/actions/setup fails with cannot find action.yml because actions/checkout runs inside the composite instead of in each job first. Move actions/checkout into each job as the first step before the composite call. Edit workflow YAML.

Read the contribution guidelines and issue templates for upstream repo mraza007/baton: CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE (any bug-report template and its required fields/labels), README contributing section, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, and any issue-filing conventions. Read-only GitHub query to prepare filing a bug report.

Assess activity and maintenance health of upstream GitHub repo mraza007/baton: recent commit history and cadence, last release/tag, number of contributors, total commits, stars, open vs closed issues and PRs over time, and any sign the maintainer responds to issues. Read-only GitHub query to judge whether the project is actively maintained.

Implement Phase 0 of drift-aggregation per spec: add a timestamp field to variance records in Python. Modify save_variance_record and combine_variance in src/claude_prospector/variance_save.py to derive earliest transcript-entry timestamp from raw read_transcript dicts, and update tests in tests/unit/test_variance_save.py. Net-new code with TDD; verify via uv run pytest and uv run ruff check.

Verify the Baton orchestrator hook-firing model from mraza007/baton source: does the before_run hook fire once per run or once per continuation turn, and does after_run fire per turn? Determine whether a harness-side before_run guard could short-circuit continuation turns for a blocked issue, or whether a WORKFLOW.md prompt-level early-exit is the only viable harness-side mitigation. Read-only investigation across upstream source and our findings.

Investigate Baton symphony turn mechanics in mraza007/baton worker.py and orchestrator.py: does each continuation turn spawn a fresh claude process, or does Baton resume a session (--resume/--continue or a captured session_id from prior turn JSON)? What prompt is passed per turn (rendered WORKFLOW.md body on turn 1 vs hardcoded continuation string on turns 2+)? What state carries across turns - model conversation memory, worktree files, git, issue labels? Read-only source investigation.

Lower agent.max_turns from 3 to 2 in config/WORKFLOW.md front matter as the block-cost bound, and fix the inaccurate docstring in src/baton_harness/before_run.py that claims the hook fires before each agent run turn (it fires once before turn 1). Small config + docstring edit with pytest verification.

Port two accumulated snapshot UI changes from prototype-feed.html into the real Jinja prototype source: a New freshness flag promoted to a leading solid-accent marker on the job-card title, and a New only filter toggle in the feed subbar that hides non-new cards via JS, wired to a real is_new listing flag / query param server-side. Edit Jinja templates, CSS, and Python; add tests; verify with ruff and pytest.

Fix CI Lint failure on PR #218: run uv run ruff format to reformat two Python files src/claude_prospector/cli/variance_save.py and tests/unit/test_variance_save.py that ruff format --check flagged, then commit and push to the PR branch. Mechanical formatter fix, no logic change.

Plan a new feature for baton-harness: autonomous dependency-chain orchestration across GitHub issues. Create a feature branch, find first ready issue, branch off the feature branch, implement, merge into feature branch, unblock dependents via GitHub dependency chain, mark next ready, repeat. Needs scoping and a central architecture decision (build harness-side around dormant Baton vs fork Baton vs separate chain driver). Produce a project plan/spec for issue #27.

Implement issue #160 (F-2): recalibrate two health threshold constants in src/claude_wayfinder/_health/_metrics.py — change _UNWANTED_BYPASS_SHARE_MAX from 0.50 to 0.20 and _UNKNOWN_SHARE_WARN from 0.10 to 0.05. Update the bootstrap comments. Update tests/test_health.py which asserts on these constant values and PASS/WARN rendering. Python code change, behavior-preserving config recalibration, must pass ruff and full pytest suite.

Deep-dive the cloned external repo mraza007/baton (package symphony) to answer three architecture questions for baton-harness #27: (1) shape of Baton entrypoints and how we would call them, (2) how much of our dependency-chain plan duplicates Baton, (3) whether to integrate/vendor parts of Baton vs wrap it. Read the actual source files in full (orchestrator, poller, worker loop, hooks, config loading, CLI). Produce an architecture analysis and recommendation. No failure is being diagnosed; this is code comprehension + build-vs-fork judgment.

Investigate a suspected bug in remote GitHub repo nexu-io/open-design via GitHub API (no local checkout): when using the Claude Code local CLI runner, when Claude emits a user-facing AskUserQuestion interactive prompt with selections, the Open-Design runner does not block/wait for the user response and continues executing agent instructions. Root cause unknown and may span the Open-Design runner harness integration or the Claude Code CLI itself. Read the runner code that integrates the Claude Code CLI, determine how it parses streams Claude output and whether it blocks for interactive question/permission prompts, localize the likely root cause layer, search existing issues and PRs for duplicates, then prepare a bug report following the repo CONTRIBUTING guidelines and issue templates. Failure diagnosis spanning code and tooling integration, root cause not yet localized.

Implement Phase 1 drift-report CLI per spec: new Python module src/claude_prospector/cli/drift_report.py with build_parser and run, register in __main__.py, add tests/unit/test_drift_report.py. Net-new feature with pure aggregation logic, window parsing, JSON and text output. TDD; verify via uv run pytest and uv run ruff.

Add documentation prose about uv.lock maintenance to README.md Contributing section and docs/maintenance/release-process.md. Explain that uv.lock is now tracked and must be regenerated with uv lock and committed whenever pyproject.toml dependencies change. Docs-only prose edit, no code.
Read-only GitHub query: list open issues and open PRs for the repo, and read the current state (open/closed, title, body) of issues #316 and #161. Return raw results.
Read-only GitHub state for whats-next recap: list open issues and open PRs for glitchwerks/claude-configs, grouped and summarized, flag deferred follow-ups. Pure read query.

Quick feasibility investigation of an external GitHub issue at nexu-io/open-design issue 3808. Read the issue to understand what is being asked, then explore the open-design codebase to assess how hard it would be to implement. Read-only scoping and investigation, no failure observed, no code changes yet. User wants to know whether they can reasonably take a stab at it. Involves reading issue text, searching and exploring an unfamiliar external repo, evaluating effort.

Investigate root cause: why do ops read queries (list issues, read PR, check CI) consistently bypass the dispatch skill and reach the ops agent router-direct without going through dispatch first? Failure diagnosis spanning the dispatch matcher, the bypass-taxonomy hook signal attribution, and router-drift telemetry data. Root cause unknown. Confirm or rule out hook timing/attribution race, matcher coverage of ops-read phrasing, router habit, and skill-mediated invocations. Produce a root-cause report with a scoped recommendation. No code change in this investigation.

Scope out the potential difficulty of GitHub issue nexu-io/open-design#3808 before deciding whether to tackle it. Read the issue body, understand what the work entails, explore the relevant code in the open-design monorepo, and assess implementation difficulty and effort. No code changes — read-only investigation and effort estimation.

Investigate whether the AskUserQuestion pause bug in open-design can be fixed from the open-design daemon side, or whether its interaction model with the headless claude process fundamentally cannot pause mid-turn for a host answer. Trace the actual stdin lifecycle, stream-json input format, pendingHostAnswers tracking, and tool-result delivery path in apps/daemon to determine if the answer is genuinely delivered back to a live claude process and the turn resumes. Read-only root-cause investigation spanning the open-design and claude code boundary.

Answer a Claude Code capability question: does the headless claude -p / Claude Agent SDK mode with --input-format stream-json support answering an interactive AskUserQuestion tool call (or any tool) by injecting a tool_result block back into stdin mid-turn, keeping the same turn alive so the model resumes with the answer? Or does headless mode auto-resolve or error AskUserQuestion such that no externally-supplied host answer can land? Need authoritative confirmation from Claude Code / Agent SDK documentation.

Run a live empirical repro: invoke headless claude -p with --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json, send a prompt that triggers an AskUserQuestion tool call, capture the JSONL output, and inspect whether the assistant message carrying the tool_use has stop_reason tool_use (turn holds open, Scenario A) or end_turn (turn auto-advances, Scenario B). Iterative manual verification to resolve the crux of an architectural feasibility question. Read-only probe, no repo changes.

Empirically test whether headless claude can have the AskUserQuestion tool removed from its toolset via a CLI flag (disallowedTools/allowedTools) so the model never emits the tool call and runs straight through, versus merely denying it which still triggers the auto-error. Run probes and inspect the init tools list and whether AskUserQuestion tool_use still appears. Read-only investigation, no repo changes.

Read GitHub pull request nexu-io/open-design#3833 and report what the author is trying to do: fetch the PR title, body, linked issue, list of changed files with diffs, and any review comments or CI status. Read-only GitHub query. Summarize their approach to the AskUserQuestion / issue #3808 problem.

Post a comment on GitHub issue nexu-io/open-design#3808 documenting the empirical investigation findings: that headless claude auto-errors AskUserQuestion and ends the turn, the stream-json answer mechanism does not work as intended, how this relates to PR #3833, and the --disallowedTools AskUserQuestion option. GitHub write under the user identity, router-handled.

Audit the codebase for every place that compares or matches a skill name, to find all sites with the same bare-skill-name vs plugin-namespaced-skill-name (claude-wayfinder:dispatch) fragility as the one fixed in #322. Verify whether the fix should normalize the plugin namespace dynamically rather than hardcode the namespaced literal. Check consistency of skill-name handling across the JS hooks and the Python matcher. Read-only consistency/conformance audit producing a report and recommendation. No failure observed beyond the one known case.

Refactor and extend the fix on an existing branch: introduce a shared bareSkillName(name) normalization helper in hooks/lib/skill-name.js, refactor the two existing dispatch-sentinel comparison sites onto it (replacing the hardcoded claude-wayfinder:dispatch literal), and fix the live INTERACTIVE_SKILLS membership bug at bypass-taxonomy.js:97 by normalizing the skill name before the set lookup. Update tests. Behavior fix plus behavior-preserving consolidation of existing JS hook code.

Edit the hook-authoring skill SKILL.md harness file to add a footgun callout about the Skill tool skill-name value being plugin-namespaced vs bare, plus a self-check item. Harness edit to a skill definition.

Port accumulated Roles-page design changes from snapshot prototype-roles.html into real Jinja source: title include/exclude become add/remove entry lists, scoring notes becomes fixed-height scrollable textarea with popout modal, per-role Match threshold field removed, tier bands get flex-shrink:0 to fix clipping. Multi-file Jinja+CSS+Python port, write tests, run pytest and ruff.
Find and read the context finder GitHub issue in the current repo, then scope it out for implementation. Read-only GitHub issue lookup followed by feature scoping and planning.

Produce a design doc / ADR that resolves the open design questions for a new context-injection feature: a lighter-weight unit of guidance the dispatch matcher can attach to a decision. Scope the design, challenge assumptions, work through tradeoffs (injection mechanism, scoring threshold, decision-ladder interaction, delineation vs skills/overrides/memory, budget discipline, authoring surface), and break it into follow-up issues. This is up-front design and planning, not coding.

Analyze and discuss the failure states of the Baton orchestrator system. Identify where things can go wrong across the orchestration flow - worker runs, GitHub issue processing, Claude Code agent execution, the vendored symphony orchestrator, turn loops, blocked-issue handling - and design how each failure should be detected and handled. Exploratory design and analysis of failure modes, error handling, resilience, architecture review.

File four GitHub tracking issues under a new Failure-mode hardening milestone for the baton-harness orchestrator. Create issues and a milestone via GitHub write tools. Each issue cites failure-mode findings. Create issue, GitHub write, tracking, milestone.

Investigate how the baton-harness uses the GitHub PAT (gh_pat) and design a startup validation that computes the minimal fine-grained PAT permissions needed and refuses to run if the provided PAT is more permissive than the minimum. Security hardening, least-privilege design, advisory and exploratory before implementation.

Implement a new Python module _auth.py exposing validate_github_token() that performs a GitHub PAT token-type gate (reject non github_pat_ fine-grained tokens) plus a startup capability self-test via the gh CLI, called at the top of before_run.main(), failing fast with a clear error. Write full Google-style docstrings, type hints, and pytest unit tests patching subprocess/gh per the existing after_run test pattern. Net-new code implementation in an existing Python package.

Write a concise security section in the README/runbook documenting the minimal fine-grained GitHub PAT grant and the bot-account least-privilege recommendation with the explicit defense-in-depth caveat. Prose documentation only, no code.
Read GitHub issue glitchwerks/claude-wayfinder#342 to understand task scope before planning work. GitHub read-only query.
